Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Conflict of Interest Division 
J Street, Suite 522 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Lowenstein: 
October 22, 1976 
As Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Sacramento Area Economic 
Opportunity Council, I would appreciate receiving an opinion. 
The local press has raised a question of conflict of interest with 
respect to the services of the SAEOC Legal Counsel. 
Mr. James Long is 
retained by the agency and has represented us in numerous legal proceedings. 
The press has raised the issue of conflict of interest because Mr. Long 
represented Mr. John Robinson in a bankruptcy case and also represented the 
agency in a personnel litigation in which Mr. Robinson was one of the 
defendents. 
To the best of my ~~owledge the bankruptcy resulted from financial 
problems encumbered prior to his employment by SAEOC. 
The personnel liti-
gation involved allegations by one Alice Coburn that she was terminated 
owing to her accusations of alleged fiscal improprieties involving Mr. 
Robinson. 
Subsequent to her allegations, her firing, and her loss in 
court, Mr. Robinson was indicted and convicted of embezzlement. 
Mr. Long believes there was no conflict of interest. 
An opinion is 
desired. 
We, the Board of Directors, would appreciate an opinion as 
quickly as possible so that we may put the matter to rest one way or the 
other. 
Thank you for your consideration. 
Sincerely, 
Dr. Malcolm Richland 
White Oak Court 
Sacramento, CA

sections.question: Whether James Long, retained as Legal Counsel by the Sacramento Area Economic Opportunity Council (SAEOC), has a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 where he represented both Mr. John Robinson in a bankruptcy case and represented the agency in personnel litigation against Mr. Robinson.
sections.conclusion: Accordingly, we cannot conclude that an attorney is a 'consultant' within the meaning of the regulation and hence is not subject to the prohibitions of Government Code Section 87100.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a retained legal counsel for a government agency have a conflict of interest when representing both the agency and an individual involved in litigation with that agency?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the attorney does not qualify as a 'consultant' under the conflict of interest regulations because he has no retainer, no authority beyond providing legal advice, and no real or apparent authority to act on behalf of the agency or bind it to a specific course of action.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant OCR degradation and character loss throughout. Both the incoming request (dated October 22, 1976) and FPPC response (dated January 7, 1977) are present. Some statutory text and regulatory analysis sections are severely garbled making complete reconstruction difficult. The core conclusion is clear but supporting legal analysis is partially obscured.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether James Long, retained as Legal Counsel by the Sacramento Area Economic Opportunity Council (SAEOC), has a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 where he represented both Mr. John Robinson in a bankruptcy case and represented the agency in personnel litigation against Mr. Robinson.

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, we cannot conclude that an attorney is a 'consultant' within the meaning of the regulation and hence is not subject to the prohibitions of Government Code Section 87100.

The local press has raised a question of conflict of interest with respect to the services of the SAEOC Legal Counsel. Mr. James Long is retained by the agency and has represented us in numerous legal proceedings. embedding.summary: An FPPC advice letter responding to Dr. Malcolm Richland's inquiry about whether the Sacramento Area Economic Opportunity Council's legal counsel has a conflict of interest for representing both the agency and an individual in separate matters. The FPPC determined the attorney does not qualify as a 'consultant' subject to conflict of interest prohibitions.
